commit.message:
Fix: Mobile streaming and UI issues

Fixes three UX issues:

1. Sections button wasn't functional - added click handler to toggle section visibility
2. Streaming disconnects on mobile tab switch - added page visibility event listener
3. Page refresh loses stream connection - added running scan detection on page load

New features:
- Sections button (📑) now toggles section visibility when clicked
- Page visibility API detects when user switches tabs (helps debugging)
- /api/scans/<repo_name> endpoint now returns running_experiment status
- On page load, checks if a scan is running and notifies user
- User can restart scan to reconnect if connection was lost

Improvements:
- Better mobile experience for long-running scans
- Clear messaging when scans are in progress after page reload
- Sections UI can now be toggled during scan results review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: restore NPM_TOKEN auth and add --access public for scoped package publish (#7475)

* fix: explicitly dispatch CI after tag push to build release binaries

GITHUB_TOKEN pushes do not trigger new workflow runs (GitHub prevents
recursive loop triggers). This means the tag push from the release
workflow never triggered osrm-backend.yml, so no binaries were built
or uploaded to the release assets.

Changes:
- Add workflow_dispatch trigger to osrm-backend.yml
- Add 'actions: write' permission to release workflow
- After pushing the tag, explicitly dispatch osrm-backend.yml on the
  tag ref; CI runs with GITHUB_REF=refs/tags/v* so PUBLISH=On and
  prebuilt binaries are built and uploaded
- Restore NODE_AUTH_TOKEN and add --access public for scoped npm package

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove redundant NPM_TOKEN and --access public from publish step

publishConfig.access=public is already set in package.json, so --access
public is redundant. OIDC via setup-node handles authentication when
id-token: write is set.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(cli): sanitize NaN/Infinity floats in JSON output for RFC 8259 compliance

- Add _sanitize_for_json() helper in cli.py: recursively replaces
  non-finite floats (nan, inf, -inf) with None before json.dumps()
- Apply sanitizer in _write_json_output() and _run_gate() JSON paths
- Replace float('inf') sentinel in _create_error_analysis() with 999.0
- Update avg_inflation filter to use status=='error' instead of inf check
- Root cause: _create_error_analysis() stored float('inf') which Python
  json.dumps() serializes as 'Infinity' (invalid per RFC 8259), causing
  jq parse error (exit code 5) in CI self-slop detection job

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

START.bat: neue Option 0 fuer Mod-Installation, kein Neustart-Blocker

- Option 0 = Nur Mod installieren (installiert ZIP + klare Anweisung)

Erster Mod-Update-Ablauf:
  START.bat -> 0 (Mod installieren) -> BeamNG neu starten

国内市场（china）板块：

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: write valid ironclaw.yaml before spawning to fix memory backend crash

IronClaw's built-in secure defaults set memory.backend = 'sqlite' but
the binary only accepts 'encrypted_sqlite', 'file', or 'none', causing
an immediate exit with 'Unknown memory backend: sqlite'.

- GatewayService.kt: inject a minimal ironclaw.yaml (memory.backend:
  encrypted_sqlite) before 'ironclaw run --ui' if none exists
- provider_config_service.dart: include memory.backend: encrypted_sqlite
  in the config generated by the provider setup wizard

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: stabilize flaky Fluent UI Dialog portal tests

Replace userEvent.click() with fireEvent.click() for dialog-trigger
clicks in agentConfigCard, templateCard, and settingsPanel tests.

Root cause: userEvent simulates a full pointer/mouse/focus event
sequence that doesn't reliably trigger React state updates for
dialog-opening clicks under parallel test load in jsdom. The
multi-event simulation creates timing windows where the Portal
mount can be delayed past the findByRole timeout.

fireEvent.click() dispatches a single synchronous click event,
which deterministically triggers the onClick handler and React
state update. Dialog content interactions (clicking buttons within
the dialog) still use userEvent.click() since the portal is already
mounted by that point.

Also: use within(dialog).getByRole() to scope button queries to
the dialog portal, and configure asyncUtilTimeout for safety margin.

Verified: 20/20 consecutive full-suite runs (1835 tests each) pass.
Previously failed ~30% of runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(scaffolder): add AdoOrganizationPicker and AdoProjectPicker field extensions

Two new custom ScaffolderFieldExtensions replacing free-text ADO fields:

AdoOrganizationPicker:
  - Two-step ADO API: GET /profile/profiles/me → GET /accounts?memberId={id}
  - Lists all organizations the user belongs to
  - Emits the organization name (used in ADO URLs)
  - 14 TDD tests (written first)

AdoProjectPicker:
  - GET dev.azure.com/{org}/_apis/projects?api-version=7.1
  - Lists projects in the selected organization
  - Reads org from formContext via configurable ui:options.organizationFieldName
    (default: 'ado_organization') — re-fetches on org change
  - Shows disabled placeholder when no org is selected
  - Emits the project name
  - 15 TDD tests (written first)

Both use microsoftAuthApiRef with ADO scope:
  499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798/user_impersonation

Both have error fallback to plain text input.

scaffolder/index.ts refactored to a makeExtension helper (DRY).

commit.message:
Complete plan-50: Fix cancelled pickups not returning to the available helper pool

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: revert amidi timeout from 15s to 2s

amidi -t is a recording duration, not a deadline — setting it to 15s
caused every request to take the full 15 seconds to complete.

The BTS 15s timeout is a JavaScript async task watchdog (per-task
deadline in a queue), fundamentally different from amidi's -t flag
which controls how long the subprocess records incoming MIDI data.

2s gives adequate margin over typical device response time (~100-200ms)
while keeping load times fast.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore(Docker): add Dockerfile; allow dist to be committed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: support multiple OIDC issuers for mobile app tokens

The GT3 app uses a separate OIDC application (gt3-companion) on the same
Authentik instance. Its tokens have a different 'iss' claim than the
server's primary OIDC issuer (fluxhaus), causing JWT validation to fail
with 'unexpected iss claim value'.

Add OIDC_ADDITIONAL_ISSUERS env var (comma-separated) to accept tokens
from additional trusted issuers that share the same JWKS signing keys.

fix: move stats grid outside loading guard so it's always visible

Stats grid now renders immediately with 0 defaults, independent of the
loading state. This fixes the E2E test that checks for 'Want to Play'
text, which was blocked by loading=true never resolving in CI.
Also a UX improvement - users see stats structure immediately.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: don't push subminor version tag on scheduled builds (#64)

During scheduled (cron) runs github.ref_type=='branch' and
github.ref_name=='main' are both true, so the previous condition
  (github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.ref_name == 'main') || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
incorrectly enabled the exact version tag (e.g. 2.0.8) on every
nightly build, re-pushing an immutable tag that should only be
created once.

Fix: key the enable flag on the event name instead.
- workflow_run  → new commit landed on main, push all tags including subminor
- workflow_dispatch → manual run, keep subminor for convenience
- schedule → nightly security-rebuild, push only :latest :major :major.minor and SHA

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(scaffolder): add AzureGroupPicker custom field extension with TDD tests

Replaces the free-text aks_admin_group_object_id UUID input with a dropdown
listing all Azure AD groups the logged-in user is a member of (including
nested/transitive memberships).

Uses the existing Microsoft SSO session (microsoftAuthApiRef) to obtain a
Microsoft Graph token via the GroupMember.Read.All delegated scope.
The browser calls the Graph API directly (CORS supported — no backend proxy).

Display format: 'Group Name (object-id)'
Emitted value:  Group Object ID UUID only

Fallback: if the Graph fetch fails, degrades to a plain text input
with UUID pattern validation.

New files:
  packages/app/src/scaffolder/AzureGroupPicker.tsx
  packages/app/src/scaffolder/AzureGroupPicker.test.tsx  (13 tests, TDD-first)

Modified:
  packages/app/src/scaffolder/index.ts  (add AzureGroupPicker to scaffolderModule)
  packages/app/src/scaffolder/AzureSubscriptionPicker.test.tsx  (fix sessionState$ mock type)

Prerequisite (one-time, done by an admin):
  Azure Portal > App registrations > Backstage app > API permissions
  Add: Microsoft Graph > GroupMember.Read.All (Delegated)
  Grant admin consent.

commit.message:
Improve MIDI transport read performance based on BTS analysis

- Add BTS byte-rate pacing (msgBytes*1000/3125 + 20ms DeviceInterval)
  before every send/request in KatanaSession to prevent device flooding
- Raise DefaultRequestTimeout from 1.5s to 15s to match BTS ProductSetting.timeout
- Widen MaximumSpanLength from 8 to 32 bytes (BTS uses SYSEX_MAXLEN=128);
  this batches ChainPattern + variation colors (0x39-3D) into 1 read
  instead of 4 separate requests
- Reduce ReadSyncInterval from 4s to 2s (phases are faster with less I/O)
- Extract ApplyDeviceState() helper to centralize suppressChangeTracking
  guard, replacing 9 scattered try/finally blocks
- Add exponential backoff on write flush failures:
  min(100 * 2^retryCount, 2000)ms before re-arming the write timer

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(logging): add OTEL disable env var overrides (issue #400) (#401)

* fix(logging): add OTEL disable env var overrides (issue #400)

Add OTEL_SDK_DISABLED, OTEL_LOGS_DISABLED, OTEL_TRACES_DISABLED, and
OTEL_METRICS_DISABLED env var overrides to applyDefaults in
libs/go/logging. These take priority over Config field values, allowing
operators to disable OTEL signals at runtime without code changes.

OTEL_SDK_DISABLED follows the OpenTelemetry spec and disables all three
signals. When the OTLP collector is unavailable (e.g. bare-metal hosts),
setting OTEL_SDK_DISABLED=true prevents the exporter from dialling
127.0.0.1:4317, eliminating the log buffer saturation and cascading
RabbitMQ heartbeat loss described in the bug.

Closes #400

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/whale-net/everything/sessions/460645f9-c02a-4bd4-9943-123d1e9318dc

Co-authored-by: QMasterMoo <3915399+QMasterMoo@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Docs/investments chart decoder playbook phase (#190)

* docs: add investments chart decoder and playbook phase spec

* docs: add Copilot prompt for investments chart decoder and playbook phase

* feat: Investments chart decoder and playbook phase — full implementation

- Extended types.ts with PlaybookEntry, PositionEvent, PlaybookReflection,
  ChartAnalysis, ChartDecoderResult, LifetimePL, and supporting enums
- Extended investment-service.ts with full CRUD for playbook entries,
  position events, reflections, chart analyses, and lifetime P/L computation
- Extended use-investments.ts hook with all new operations
- Created PlaybookPanel, PlaybookEntryForm, PositionEventTable,
  ReflectionPanel, PerformanceSummaryCard frontend components
- Created ChartDecoderUploader, ChartAnalysisCard, ChartAnalysisDetail
  frontend components for chart upload and analysis display
- Updated InvestmentsPage with 4 tabs: Holdings, Watchlist, Playbook,
  Chart Decoder — plus overall P/L summary in the header
- Created chart-decoder.js Netlify function with multimodal OpenAI
  analysis and structured fallback
- Created investment-playbook.js Netlify function with full CRUD for
  entries, events, reflections, P/L computation, and chart linking
- Created 040_investments_playbook_chart_decoder.sql Supabase migration
  with all tables, indexes, RLS policies, and FK cascade
- Added 21 tests for playbook, events, reflections, chart analyses,
  and P/L computation (all pass)
